mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for corporate earnings, mechanically increasing present values and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by >0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where the unemployment rate rises above the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP and month-over-month increases in retail sales confirm robust aggregate demand, which drives corporate revenue expansion and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect accelerating manufacturing and utility output, which triggers short-term momentum buying and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of cases during recovery phases, or series autocorrelation explains the returns instead of the macro signal.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a suppressed federal funds rate compresses real yields and forces markets to price in aggressive future tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where inflation exceeds 230 and rates remain below 0.5%, or the real rate differential shows no predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the labor force and supports wage growth, which increases corporate profit margins and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The payroll growth signal yields a negative rank IC over any 24-month rolling window, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero during high-growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5% signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and construction activity, which reduces capital goods demand and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The housing starts threshold produces positive forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive accuracy when adjusted for seasonal factors.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in industrial production and retail sales indicate synchronized supply-side expansion and demand absorption, which triggers algorithmic momentum strategies and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over 5-day horizons in more than 70% of test cases, or the interaction effect vanishes after controlling for market volatility.
